headline: Wealthfront raises $484.6M in IPO on Nasdaq at $14 per share, validating the AI-driven robo-advisor model at public market scale
the_so_what: A $484M public market debut for a 0.25%-fee automated wealth platform is a direct signal to every full-service institution that the mass affluent market is being permanently repriced by AI — not gradually, but structurally.
summary: Wealthfront priced its IPO at $14.00 per share — the top of its $12–$14 range — and began trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under ticker WLTH on December 12, 2025. The offering raised approximately $300.6M for the company from 21.5M new shares, with total proceeds including selling stockholders reaching $484.6M. Goldman Sachs and J.P. Morgan acted as lead underwriters. Wealthfront describes itself as a 'tech-driven financial platform helping digital natives turn their savings into wealth', with its core proposition built on AI-driven portfolio management, automated tax-loss harvesting, and continuous rebalancing at a 0.25% advisory fee.

key_stat.label: Raised in Wealthfront IPO on Nasdaq (December 2025) — ticker: WLTH

verified_claims: IPO priced at $14.00 per share — top of $12–$14 range (GlobeNewswire official press release December 12, 2025)
Began trading on Nasdaq Global Select Market as WLTH on December 12, 2025 (press release)
21,538,461 shares offered by the company (press release)
Gross proceeds to Wealthfront approximately $300.6M (press release)
Total proceeds including selling stockholders: $484.6M (press release)
Goldman Sachs and J.P. Morgan as lead underwriters (press release)
30-day greenshoe option for up to 5,192,308 additional shares (press release)
_governance.notes: All claims sourced directly from GlobeNewswire official press release dated December 12, 2025. human_approved: Track 2 entry written directly from verified source.
